query: Create a price oracle demo that fetches ETH prices from multiple sources
expected_behavior: Adds reqwest dependency to workspace Cargo.toml and helloworld Cargo.toml
Creates a GET /price/{asset} route handler in router.rs
Implements fetching from multiple price sources
Uses proper error handling (no unwrap/expect in production code)
Computes median or aggregated price
Returns JSON with asset, price, source count, and timestamp

query: Add a compliance check endpoint that screens trades against a sanctions list
expected_behavior: Creates a POST /trade/check route handler
Accepts trade details as JSON (instrument, counterparty, amount)
Checks counterparty against a configurable sanctions list
Returns APPROVE or REJECT with detailed check results
Includes the specific check that caused rejection on DENY
Writes unit tests for both passing and failing trade checks

query: Write e2e tests for a new /auction/create endpoint
expected_behavior: Creates a new test file in src/e2e/tests/
Uses the e2e::Builder pattern to spawn the server
Makes real HTTP requests with reqwest
Tests the happy path (valid auction creation)
Tests error cases (missing required fields)
Uses the TestArgs.base_url for constructing request URLs

query: I need to build a demo that does policy evaluation but the logic is complex enough to warrant its own crate
expected_behavior: Creates a new crate in the workspace with cargo new --lib
Adds the new crate to src/Cargo.toml workspace members
Adds the new crate as a dependency in helloworld/Cargo.toml
Implements business logic in the new crate
Uses the new crate from router.rs handlers
Follows workspace lint rules (no unsafe, no unwrap)

query: Build and deploy my demo to TVC
expected_behavior: Runs make -C src test and make -C src lint first
Builds the OCI container with make out/helloworld/index.json
Explains how to compute the binary digest for the deployment template
Shows the tvc app create and tvc deploy create commands
Mentions the quorum approval step

query: Create a sealed-bid auction demo for both DeFi and TradFi audiences
expected_behavior: Creates endpoints for auction creation, bid submission, and reveal
Uses in-memory storage for auctions and bids
Rejects bids after the auction deadline
On reveal, determines winner and returns all bids for transparency
Explains the value proposition for both web3 and bank audiences
Writes tests covering the full auction lifecycle
